bonuses.leadin: This painting prefigures a later series with the same title, which consists of gouache-painted cutouts modeled on the artist’s earlier painting Joy of Life. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting inspired by a sculpture the artist saw in the Algerian city of Biskra. This painting was burned in effigy at the Armory Show.

This artist quipped that Matisse could “paint a nude or do a design,” but not both, in response to Matisse’s The Blue Nude. This artist painted a blue, nude version of his mistress Marie-Thérèse Walter, as well as The Blue Room.

This later series of artworks was created by painting nude models blue and having them roll around on canvases as “living brushes.” A 20 minute film showing the making of paintings in this series is accompanied by a single D-flat chord called the “Monotone Silence Symphony.”
answers: Blue Nude (Souvenir de Biskra) [or Nu bleu, Souvenir de Biskra; accept Blue Nudes or Nues bleus]
answers_sanitized: Blue Nude (Souvenir de Biskra) [or Nu bleu, Souvenir de Biskra; accept Blue Nudes or Nues bleus]

parts_sanitized: Name this painting inspired by a sculpture the artist saw in the Algerian city of Biskra. This painting was burned in effigy at the Armory Show.

This artist quipped that Matisse could "paint a nude or do a design," but not both, in response to Matisse's The Blue Nude. This artist painted a blue, nude version of his mistress Marie-Therese Walter, as well as The Blue Room.

This later series of artworks was created by painting nude models blue and having them roll around on canvases as "living brushes." A 20 minute film showing the making of paintings in this series is accompanied by a single D-flat chord called the "Monotone Silence Symphony."
